notes: spec-debate is a stateful, tool-driven skill that invokes the external Codex CLI and edits a target spec file in place. The most meaningful test is a functional end-to-end dogfood (round 1 then round 2), not a baseline-vs-skill benchmark — there is no sensible 'no-skill' baseline since without the skill the agent would not call Codex, vet findings, or persist debate state. These prompts capture realistic invocations for regression checks. Evals 1-2 assume the referenced spec file already exists; create a short throwaway spec at that path first — these are dogfood runs, not shipped fixtures.

evals.prompt: I've just finished the spec for an internal URL-shortener service (/tmp/spec-debate-test/url-shortener-spec.md). Run it through a debate with codex and improve it.
evals.expected_output: Round 1: Claude and codex each independently propose improvements (codex does not see Claude's current-round list); Claude merges both lists with veto (verifying against the actual file, not blindly), applies accepted ones via Edit, writes a skimmable report (accepted/partial/rejected with one-line reasons and source codex|own — rejected own proposals included), and creates .url-shortener-spec.md.debate-state.json with round 1 recorded.
expectations: A Codex critique ran via scripts/run_codex_critique.sh (exactly one codex exec at a time)
Claude listed its own proposals independently, and they went through the same verdict procedure as Codex's
Every finding received accepted/partial/rejected with a one-line reason; Claude's own proposals went through the same vetting (rejected own proposals are reported whenever they occur)
Accepted/partial edits were applied to the spec file via Edit, in the spec's voice
.url-shortener-spec.md.debate-state.json was created beside the spec and parses as a single valid JSON document with round 1 recorded

evals.prompt: Do one more iteration of improvements on the spec.
evals.expected_output: Round 2: skill reads the existing .url-shortener-spec.md.debate-state.json, sets round=2, passes settled (rejected/partial) verdicts to codex so they are not re-raised, gathers fresh proposals from both models, merges with veto, applies accepted edits, reports with convergence status, and appends round 2 to the state file.
expectations: The existing debate-state file was loaded and the new round numbered 2
Settled rejected/partial verdicts from round 1 were passed to Codex as not-to-re-raise
Fresh proposals were gathered from both models and merged with veto
The report includes a convergence assessment (recommend STOP or what a next round would target)
Round 2 was appended and the state file still parses as one valid JSON document

evals.prompt: let codex stress-test this design doc with maximum reasoning depth
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, resolves the spec from context, parses 'maximum reasoning depth' as xhigh, runs codex at xhigh, and completes a debate round. Tests effort-argument parsing and triggering on casual phrasing without the word 'debate'.
expectations: The skill triggered without the word 'debate' in the prompt
'maximum reasoning depth' was parsed as effort xhigh and passed to the helper
The spec was resolved from the conversation context rather than guessed from the filesystem
A full debate round completed with the merge-with-veto report
